summary: Gruber presents web traffic data from Daring Fireball and other sites showing that the iPad, only six weeks after launch and available only in the U.S., already generates more web traffic than all Android devices combined. He compares these findings with data from TechCrunch, Business Insider, and Tim Bray's blog, all showing similar patterns. Despite NPD reporting that Android phones outsold iPhones in the previous quarter, Android's web traffic share remains negligibly small. Gruber finds this discrepancy puzzling, especially since Android phones have good browsers and large screens, unlike BlackBerrys whose low web usage could be attributed to poor browsing experiences.
key_points: The iPad accounted for 5.85% of Daring Fireball visits in its first six weeks, rising to 9.16% on weekends, while Android registered only 0.21%
Multiple sites (TechCrunch, Business Insider, Tim Bray's blog) show similar patterns of iPad outpacing Android in web traffic despite Android's larger installed base
Android phones outsold iPhones in Q1 2010 according to NPD, yet Android's web traffic presence remains negligible — a contradiction Gruber can't explain
BlackBerry's low web presence could be blamed on poor browsers and small screens, but Android has neither of those excuses
iPad web traffic share was growing rapidly week over week, suggesting an engaged user base from the start
money_quotes: 5.85 percent of visits for the iPad isn't bad for a device that's only been shipping for six weeks and still isn't available outside the U.S.
I'm not sure how Android's relatively low share of web traffic jibes with NPD's report last week that Android phones outsold the iPhone last quarter.
It's certainly possible that Android users are more like BlackBerry users than iPhone users.
Android phones have iPhone-size displays and a very good WebKit-based browser.
But then why has the iPad — only on sale for six weeks, only in the U.S. — already surpassed Android? I don't get it.
key_insight: The iPad surpassing Android in web traffic within six weeks of launch, despite Android's larger phone sales, suggests Apple users engage with the web far more intensively than Android users.
